---
title: Empty State Template
description: A standard layout for empty data states, zero-results, or feature onboarding.
---

import { EmptyStateTemplate } from '@nirman/components';
import Playground from '../../../components/Playground.astro';

The `EmptyStateTemplate` is a structured **Layer 3 (Template)** component designed to handle "zero-data" scenarios gracefully. It provides a consistent, centered layout for graphics, messaging, and primary calls-to-action to help users recover or proceed.

## 1. Interactive Preview

<Playground>
  <div style={{ width: '100%', padding: '24px' }}>
    <EmptyStateTemplate 
      title="No projects found" 
      description="You haven't created any projects yet. Start by creating a new project to manage your design system."
      actionText="Create new project"
      secondaryActionText="Read documentation"
      graphic="📁"
      client:load 
    />
  </div>
</Playground>

## 2. Developer Implementation

Use this template whenever a list, table, or dashboard view has no data to display.

```tsx
import { EmptyStateTemplate } from '@nirman/components';
import { FolderIcon } from 'lucide-react';

function Dashboard() {
  const hasProjects = false;

  if (!hasProjects) {
    return (
      <EmptyStateTemplate 
        graphic={<FolderIcon size={64} />}
        title="Your workspace is empty"
        description="Get started by importing your first set of design tokens."
        actionText="Import tokens"
        onAction={() => openImportModal()}
        secondaryActionText="View examples"
        onSecondaryAction={() => navigate('/examples')}
      />
    );
  }

  return <ProjectList />;
}
```

## 3. Customization & Props

| Prop | Type | Description |
| ---- | ---- | ----------- |
| `graphic` | `React.ReactNode` | Optional SVG icon, illustration, or image to render above the title. |
| `title` | `string` | The main `h2` heading explaining the state. |
| `description` | `string` | Optional descriptive text to guide the user. |
| `actionText` | `string` | Text for the primary action button. |
| `onAction` | `() => void` | Callback triggered when the primary button is clicked. |
| `secondaryActionText` | `string` | Text for an optional secondary (ghost/outline) button. |
| `onSecondaryAction` | `() => void` | Callback triggered when the secondary button is clicked. |
